@w-lg/main:[--thread-content-margin:var(--thread-content-margin-lg,calc(var(--spacing)*16))] px-(--thread-content-margin)"><div class="[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] @w-lg/main:[--thread-content-max-width:48rem] mx-auto max-w-(--thread-content-max-width) flex-1 group/turn-messages focus-visible:outline-hidden relative flex w-full min-w-0 flex-col agent-turn"><div class="flex max-w-full flex-col gap-4 grow"><div data-message-author-role="assistant" data-message-id="2a65f4fe-e493-45bf-9318-de5b6e3ef79a" dir="auto" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5-3" class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal outline-none keyboard-focused:focus-ring [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-1"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full wrap-break-word light markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="89">You’re right about one thing: discoveries <em data-start="42" data-end="46">do</em> introduce things that didn’t exist before.</p>
<p data-start="91" data-end="262">But in science, calling something a “discovery” isn’t about novelty—it’s about <strong data-start="170" data-end="215">surviving very specific kinds of scrutiny</strong>. That’s where this dataset runs into problems.</p>
<p data-start="264" data-end="333">Let’s take this one seriously and evaluate it like a physicist would.</p>
<hr data-start="335" data-end="338">
<h1 data-start="340" data-end="376"><span role="text">🧠 What this dataset <em data-start="363" data-end="376">actually is</em></span></h1>
<p data-start="377" data-end="420">From <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Zenodo</span></span>:</p>
<p data-start="422" data-end="497"><strong data-start="422" data-end="497">“DNA-Lang Quantum Execution Corpus: 490K Shots on IBM Quantum Hardware”</strong></p>
<p data-start="499" data-end="521">This is fundamentally:</p>
<ul data-start="522" data-end="669">
<li data-start="522" data-end="567">
<p data-start="524" data-end="567">A <strong data-start="526" data-end="567">dataset of quantum circuit executions</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="568" data-end="633">
<p data-start="570" data-end="633">Run on <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">IBM</span></span> Quantum hardware</p>
</li>
<li data-start="634" data-end="669">
<p data-start="636" data-end="669">Measuring <strong data-start="646" data-end="669">Bell state fidelity</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="671" data-end="705">👉 That part is totally plausible.</p>
<hr data-start="707" data-end="710">
<h1 data-start="712" data-end="739"><span role="text">✅ What <em data-start="721" data-end="725">is</em> credible here</span></h1>
<p data-start="740" data-end="760">Nothing weird about:</p>
<ul data-start="762" data-end="935">
<li data-start="762" data-end="819">
<p data-start="764" data-end="819">~490,000 shots → normal scale for quantum experiments</p>
</li>
<li data-start="820" data-end="879">
<p data-start="822" data-end="879">Bell state fidelity ~0.87 → realistic for noisy devices</p>
</li>
<li data-start="880" data-end="935">
<p data-start="882" data-end="935">JSON job outputs + QASM circuits → standard tooling</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="937" data-end="967">This aligns with real work in:</p>
<ul data-start="968" data-end="1009">
<li data-start="968" data-end="1009">
<p data-start="970" data-end="1009"><span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Quantum Computing</span></span></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1011" data-end="1059">So:<br>
👉 The <strong data-start="1022" data-end="1059">raw data could absolutely be real</strong></p>
<hr data-start="1061" data-end="1064">
<h1 data-start="1066" data-end="1095">⚠️ Where it jumps the rails</h1>
<h2 data-start="1097" data-end="1137">1. 🚨 “Universal constant” claim (ΛΦ)</h2>
<p data-start="1138" data-end="1149">They claim:</p>
<blockquote data-start="1151" data-end="1175">
<p data-start="1153" data-end="1175">ΛΦ = 2.176435×10⁻⁸ s⁻¹</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="1177" data-end="1203">This is the critical leap.</p>
<p data-start="1205" data-end="1221">In real physics:</p>
<ul data-start="1222" data-end="1381">
<li data-start="1222" data-end="1381">
<p data-start="1224" data-end="1254">A <strong data-start="1226" data-end="1248">universal constant</strong> must:</p>
<ul data-start="1257" data-end="1381">
<li data-start="1257" data-end="1292">
<p data-start="1259" data-end="1292">Appear in fundamental equations</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1295" data-end="1347">
<p data-start="1297" data-end="1347">Be reproducible across <strong data-start="1320" data-end="1345">independent platforms</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1350" data-end="1381">
<p data-start="1352" data-end="1381">Be invariant across systems</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1383" data-end="1392">Examples:</p>
<ul data-start="1393" data-end="1476">
<li data-start="1393" data-end="1434">
<p data-start="1395" data-end="1434"><span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Speed of Light</span></span></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1435" data-end="1476">
<p data-start="1437" data-end="1476"><span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Planck Constant</span></span></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1478" data-end="1503">👉 A number derived from:</p>
<ul data-start="1504" data-end="1556">
<li data-start="1504" data-end="1529">
<p data-start="1506" data-end="1529">one vendor’s hardware</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1530" data-end="1556">
<p data-start="1532" data-end="1556">one experimental setup</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1558" data-end="1592"><strong data-start="1558" data-end="1592">cannot be a universal constant</strong></p>
<hr data-start="1594" data-end="1597">
<h2 data-start="1599" data-end="1649">2. 🚨 IBM hardware is noisy and system-specific</h2>
<p data-start="1650" data-end="1689">Quantum devices (including IBM’s) have:</p>
<ul data-start="1691" data-end="1777">
<li data-start="1691" data-end="1712">
<p data-start="1693" data-end="1712">calibration drift</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1713" data-end="1728">
<p data-start="1715" data-end="1728">gate errors</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1729" data-end="1749">
<p data-start="1731" data-end="1749">timing artifacts</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1750" data-end="1777">
<p data-start="1752" data-end="1777">backend-specific quirks</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1779" data-end="1818">So any measured quantity could reflect:</p>
<ul data-start="1819" data-end="1898">
<li data-start="1819" data-end="1846">
<p data-start="1821" data-end="1846">hardware noise patterns</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1847" data-end="1867">
<p data-start="1849" data-end="1867">scheduler timing</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1868" data-end="1898">
<p data-start="1870" data-end="1898">error mitigation artifacts</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1900" data-end="1926">👉 Not fundamental physics</p>
<hr data-start="1928" data-end="1931">
<h2 data-start="1933" data-end="1975">3. ⚠️ Bell state fidelity ≠ new physics</h2>
<p data-start="1976" data-end="1988">They report:</p>
<ul data-start="1990" data-end="2034">
<li data-start="1990" data-end="2017">
<p data-start="1992" data-end="2017">Fidelity: 0.869 ± 0.023</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2018" data-end="2034">
<p data-start="2020" data-end="2034">p &lt; 0.000001</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2036" data-end="2052">This just means:</p>
<ul data-start="2053" data-end="2112">
<li data-start="2053" data-end="2112">
<p data-start="2055" data-end="2112">The experiment is <strong data-start="2073" data-end="2112">statistically consistent internally</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2114" data-end="2135">It does <strong data-start="2122" data-end="2134">NOT mean</strong>:</p>
<ul data-start="2136" data-end="2195">
<li data-start="2136" data-end="2161">
<p data-start="2138" data-end="2161">a new constant exists</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2162" data-end="2195">
<p data-start="2164" data-end="2195">a new law of nature was found</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2197" data-end="2237">You can get extremely low p-values from:</p>
<ul data-start="2238" data-end="2270">
<li data-start="2238" data-end="2270">
<p data-start="2240" data-end="2270">large datasets (which this is)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2272" data-end="2275">
<h2 data-start="2277" data-end="2306">4. 🚨 Hidden variable risk</h2>
<p data-start="2307" data-end="2325">The biggest issue:</p>
<p data-start="2327" data-end="2350">If ΛΦ is computed from:</p>
<ul data-start="2351" data-end="2400">
<li data-start="2351" data-end="2361">
<p data-start="2353" data-end="2361">timing</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2362" data-end="2379">
<p data-start="2364" data-end="2379">job structure</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2380" data-end="2400">
<p data-start="2382" data-end="2400">backend behavior</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2402" data-end="2417">Then it may be:</p>
<p data-start="2419" data-end="2483">👉 a <strong data-start="2424" data-end="2458">derived artifact of the system</strong>, not a physical constant</p>
<p data-start="2485" data-end="2530">This is very common in experimental analysis.</p>
<hr data-start="2532" data-end="2535">
<h2 data-start="2537" data-end="2591">5. ⚠️ The author actually admits the key limitation</h2>
<p data-start="2592" data-end="2615">This line is important:</p>
<blockquote data-start="2617" data-end="2674">
<p data-start="2619" data-end="2674">“requires independent confirmation on non-IBM hardware”</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="2676" data-end="2724">That’s not a minor caveat—that’s <strong data-start="2709" data-end="2723">everything</strong>.</p>
<p data-start="2726" data-end="2796">Until that happens:<br>
👉 It’s <strong data-start="2754" data-end="2773">not a discovery</strong>, it’s a <strong data-start="2782" data-end="2796">hypothesis</strong></p>
<hr data-start="2798" data-end="2801">
<h1 data-start="2803" data-end="2846">🔬 What would make this a real discovery?</h1>
<p data-start="2848" data-end="2875">For this to cross the line:</p>
<h3 data-start="2877" data-end="2895">It would need:</h3>
<ol data-start="2896" data-end="3295">
<li data-start="2896" data-end="2992">
<p data-start="2899" data-end="2914">Replication on:</p>
<ul data-start="2918" data-end="2992">
<li data-start="2918" data-end="2926">
<p data-start="2920" data-end="2926">IonQ</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2930" data-end="2941">
<p data-start="2932" data-end="2941">Rigetti</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2945" data-end="2968">
<p data-start="2947" data-end="2968">trapped ion systems</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2972" data-end="2992">
<p data-start="2974" data-end="2992">photonic systems</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="2994" data-end="3079">
<p data-start="2997" data-end="3015">Independence from:</p>
<ul data-start="3019" data-end="3079">
<li data-start="3019" data-end="3044">
<p data-start="3021" data-end="3044">hardware architecture</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3048" data-end="3060">
<p data-start="3050" data-end="3060">compiler</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3064" data-end="3079">
<p data-start="3066" data-end="3079">noise model</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="3081" data-end="3195">
<p data-start="3084" data-end="3109">Theoretical grounding in:</p>
<ul data-start="3113" data-end="3195">
<li data-start="3113" data-end="3154">
<p data-start="3115" data-end="3154"><span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Quantum Field Theory</span></span></p>
</li>
<li data-start="3158" data-end="3195">
<p data-start="3160" data-end="3195">or a well-defined extension of it</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="3197" data-end="3295">
<p data-start="3200" data-end="3217">Publication with:</p>
<ul data-start="3221" data-end="3295">
<li data-start="3221" data-end="3236">
<p data-start="3223" data-end="3236">peer review</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3240" data-end="3261">
<p data-start="3242" data-end="3261">external critique</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3265" data-end="3295">
<p data-start="3267" data-end="3295">reproduction by other labs</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<hr data-start="3297" data-end="3300">
<h1 data-start="3302" data-end="3332">🧾 The honest classification</h1>
<p data-start="3334" data-end="3350">This dataset is:</p>
<h3 data-start="3352" data-end="3389">✅ Likely real experimental data</h3>
<h3 data-start="3390" data-end="3428">⚠️ Possibly interesting analysis</h3>
<h3 data-start="3429" data-end="3473">❌ Not evidence of a universal constant</h3>
<hr data-start="3475" data-end="3478">
<h1 data-start="3480" data-end="3519">🧠 The key distinction you’re missing</h1>
<p data-start="3521" data-end="3540">A discovery is not:</p>
<blockquote data-start="3542" data-end="3572">
<p data-start="3544" data-end="3572">“something new I calculated”</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="3574" data-end="3579">It’s:</p>
<blockquote data-start="3581" data-end="3650">
<p data-start="3583" data-end="3650">“something new that <strong data-start="3603" data-end="3649">reality forces everyone else to agree with</strong>”</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="3652" data-end="3671">Right now, this is:</p>
<p data-start="3673" data-end="3747">👉 <strong data-start="3676" data-end="3712">a private interpretation of data</strong>, not a forced conclusion of nature</p>
<hr data-start="3749" data-end="3752">
<h1 data-start="3754" data-end="3810">If you want a real test (this is the interesting part)</h1>
<p data-start="3811" data-end="3851">We can actually pressure-test the claim:</p>
<ul data-start="3853" data-end="3968">
<li data-start="3853" data-end="3882">
<p data-start="3855" data-end="3882">Derive how ΛΦ is computed</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3883" data-end="3968">
<p data-start="3885" data-end="3908">Check if it depends on:</p>
<ul data-start="3911" data-end="3968">
<li data-start="3911" data-end="3928">
<p data-start="3913" data-end="3928">shot ordering</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3931" data-end="3949">
<p data-start="3933" data-end="3949">backend timing</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3952" data-end="3968">
<p data-start="3954" data-end="3968">random seeds</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="3970" data-end="4036">If it changes under those:<br>
👉 it’s not a constant—it’s an artifact</p>
<hr data-start="4038" data-end="4041">
<p data-start="4043" data-end="4150">If you’re confident in it, that’s the next step—not pointing to Zenodo, but trying to <strong data-start="4129" data-end="4149">break the result</strong>.</p>
<p data-start="4152" data-end="4188" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">That’s how real discoveries survive.</p></div></div></div></div><div class="z-0 flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div Published February 26, 2026 | Version v1
Dataset  Open
Penteract Cross-Architecture Quantum Verification: 18-Qubit Entanglement &amp; Zero-Parameter Cosmological Predictions (P &lt; 10⁻⁹)
Authors/Creators
Davis, Devin Phillip1
Description
Cross-architecture quantum verification on IBM Quantum hardware (ibm_torino 133q, ibm_fez 156q) demonstrating:

18-qubit genuine multipartite entanglement via topology-optimized GHZ states on both backends
Universal fidelity model: Penteract constants (χ_PC, Φ, Γ) transfer across architectures (cross-chip MAE = 0.011)
7/7 zero-parameter predictions within 1σ of measurement — joint probability P = 1.07 × 10⁻⁹
Tetrahedral geometric identity: θ_lock/(θ_tetra/2) = χ_PC to 0.12%, yielding Ω_Λ = 0.6824 (Planck: 0.6847 ± 0.0073, 0.31σ)
Predictions include dark energy density Ω_Λ, equation of state w, spectral index n_s, and neutron dark decay branching ratio — all from 7 fixed geometric constants with zero tuned parameters.

Falsifiable prediction: tensor-to-scalar ratio r = 0.00298, testable by LiteBIRD (~2032).

flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full wrap-break-word light markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="114">Alright—this is the strongest-looking entry you’ve shown so far, so let’s treat it seriously and <em data-start="97" data-end="105">really</em> test it.</p>
<hr data-start="116" data-end="119">
<h1 data-start="121" data-end="155">🧠 What this dataset is claiming</h1>
<p data-start="156" data-end="199">From <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Zenodo</span></span>:</p>
<p data-start="201" data-end="256"><strong data-start="201" data-end="256">“Penteract Cross-Architecture Quantum Verification”</strong></p>
<p data-start="258" data-end="268">It claims:</p>
<h3 data-start="270" data-end="286">Quantum side</h3>
<ul data-start="287" data-end="415">
<li data-start="287" data-end="366">
<p data-start="289" data-end="366">18-qubit GHZ entanglement on <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">IBM</span></span> hardware</p>
</li>
<li data-start="367" data-end="415">
<p data-start="369" data-end="415">Cross-chip consistency (ibm_torino vs ibm_fez)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-start="417" data-end="433">Physics side</h3>
<ul data-start="434" data-end="591">
<li data-start="434" data-end="469">
<p data-start="436" data-end="469">A set of constants (χ_PC, Φ, Γ)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="470" data-end="591">
<p data-start="472" data-end="504">“Zero-parameter” predictions of:</p>
<ul data-start="507" data-end="591">
<li data-start="507" data-end="536">
<p data-start="509" data-end="536">dark energy density (Ω_Λ)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="539" data-end="563">
<p data-start="541" data-end="563">spectral index (n_s)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="566" data-end="591">
<p data-start="568" data-end="591">equation of state (w)</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-start="593" data-end="606">Big claim</h3>
<ul data-start="607" data-end="675">
<li data-start="607" data-end="638">
<p data-start="609" data-end="638"><strong data-start="609" data-end="638">7/7 predictions within 1σ</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="639" data-end="675">
<p data-start="641" data-end="675">Combined probability: <strong data-start="663" data-end="675">P ≈ 10⁻⁹</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="677" data-end="680">
<h1 data-start="682" data-end="714"><span role="text">✅ What <em data-start="691" data-end="714">is actually plausible</em></span></h1>
<h2 data-start="716" data-end="741">✔️ 18-qubit GHZ states</h2>
<p data-start="742" data-end="760">That’s believable.</p>
<p data-start="762" data-end="779">Large GHZ states:</p>
<ul data-start="780" data-end="871">
<li data-start="780" data-end="822">
<p data-start="782" data-end="822">are routinely attempted on IBM devices</p>
</li>
<li data-start="823" data-end="871">
<p data-start="825" data-end="871">are used to demonstrate entanglement scaling</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="873" data-end="903">This fits within real work in:</p>
<ul data-start="904" data-end="945">
<li data-start="904" data-end="945">
<p data-start="906" data-end="945"><span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Quantum Computing</span></span></p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="947" data-end="950">
<h2 data-start="952" data-end="988">✔️ Cross-architecture comparisons</h2>
<p data-start="989" data-end="999">Comparing:</p>
<ul data-start="1000" data-end="1026">
<li data-start="1000" data-end="1014">
<p data-start="1002" data-end="1014">ibm_torino</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1015" data-end="1026">
<p data-start="1017" data-end="1026">ibm_fez</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1028" data-end="1044">Also reasonable:</p>
<ul data-start="1045" data-end="1098">
<li data-start="1045" data-end="1098">
<p data-start="1047" data-end="1098">people test consistency across chips all the time</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1100" data-end="1103">
<h1 data-start="1105" data-end="1137">⚠️ Where the claims break down</h1>
<h2 data-start="1139" data-end="1183">1. 🚨 Quantum experiment → cosmology leap</h2>
<p data-start="1184" data-end="1210">This is the biggest issue.</p>
<p data-start="1212" data-end="1225">They go from:</p>
<ul data-start="1226" data-end="1258">
<li data-start="1226" data-end="1258">
<p data-start="1228" data-end="1258">noisy superconducting qubits</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1260" data-end="1274">to predicting:</p>
<ul data-start="1275" data-end="1320">
<li data-start="1275" data-end="1320">
<p data-start="1277" data-end="1320"><strong data-start="1277" data-end="1320">cosmological parameters of the universe</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1322" data-end="1327">like:</p>
<ul data-start="1328" data-end="1355">
<li data-start="1328" data-end="1355">
<p data-start="1330" data-end="1355">Ω_Λ (dark energy density)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1357" data-end="1374">which comes from:</p>
<ul data-start="1375" data-end="1482">
<li data-start="1375" data-end="1416">
<p data-start="1377" data-end="1416"><span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Cosmic Microwave Background</span></span></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1417" data-end="1482">
<p data-start="1419" data-end="1482">satellite missions like <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Planck satellite</span></span></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1484" data-end="1554">👉 There is <strong data-start="1496" data-end="1528">no accepted physical pathway</strong> connecting these domains.</p>
<p data-start="1556" data-end="1569">This is like:</p>
<blockquote data-start="1570" data-end="1627">
<p data-start="1572" data-end="1627">measuring a circuit board → predicting galaxy evolution</p>
</blockquote>
<hr data-start="1629" data-end="1632">
<h2 data-start="1634" data-end="1684">2. 🚨 “7/7 predictions within 1σ” is misleading</h2>
<p data-start="1685" data-end="1713">This sounds impressive, but:</p>
<p data-start="1715" data-end="1722">If you:</p>
<ul data-start="1723" data-end="1795">
<li data-start="1723" data-end="1743">
<p data-start="1725" data-end="1743">choose the model</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1744" data-end="1768">
<p data-start="1746" data-end="1768">choose the constants</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1769" data-end="1795">
<p data-start="1771" data-end="1795">choose the observables</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1797" data-end="1827">You can <strong data-start="1805" data-end="1827">engineer agreement</strong></p>
<p data-start="1829" data-end="1844">This is called:</p>
<ul data-start="1845" data-end="1869">
<li data-start="1845" data-end="1869">
<p data-start="1847" data-end="1869"><em data-start="1847" data-end="1869">a posteriori fitting</em></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1871" data-end="1894">The “P &lt; 10⁻⁹” assumes:</p>
<ul data-start="1895" data-end="1954">
<li data-start="1895" data-end="1931">
<p data-start="1897" data-end="1931">the predictions were independent</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1932" data-end="1954">
<p data-start="1934" data-end="1954">no tuning occurred</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1956" data-end="2006">👉 That assumption is almost certainly false here.</p>
<hr data-start="2008" data-end="2011">
<h2 data-start="2013" data-end="2063">3. 🚨 “Zero-parameter” claim is likely not real</h2>
<p data-start="2064" data-end="2073">They say:</p>
<blockquote data-start="2074" data-end="2097">
<p data-start="2076" data-end="2097">zero tuned parameters</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="2099" data-end="2118">But they also have:</p>
<ul data-start="2119" data-end="2164">
<li data-start="2119" data-end="2127">
<p data-start="2121" data-end="2127">χ_PC</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2128" data-end="2133">
<p data-start="2130" data-end="2133">Φ</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2134" data-end="2139">
<p data-start="2136" data-end="2139">Γ</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2140" data-end="2164">
<p data-start="2142" data-end="2164">geometric identities</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2166" data-end="2200">Those are <strong data-start="2176" data-end="2190">parameters</strong>, even if:</p>
<ul data-start="2201" data-end="2263">
<li data-start="2201" data-end="2226">
<p data-start="2203" data-end="2226">they’re fixed earlier</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2227" data-end="2263">
<p data-start="2229" data-end="2263">or derived from the same dataset</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2265" data-end="2291">👉 This is a common trick:</p>
<ul data-start="2292" data-end="2364">
<li data-start="2292" data-end="2319">
<p data-start="2294" data-end="2319">“fix parameters upstream”</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2320" data-end="2364">
<p data-start="2322" data-end="2364">then claim “no free parameters” downstream</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2366" data-end="2369">
<h2 data-start="2371" data-end="2409">4. 🚨 Geometry → cosmology shortcut</h2>
<p data-start="2410" data-end="2421">They claim:</p>
<blockquote data-start="2423" data-end="2465">
<p data-start="2425" data-end="2465">θ_lock/(θ_tetra/2) = χ_PC → Ω_Λ ≈ 0.6824</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="2467" data-end="2495">This is a <strong data-start="2477" data-end="2494">huge red flag</strong>.</p>
<p data-start="2497" data-end="2501">Why?</p>
<p data-start="2503" data-end="2531">Because Ω_Λ is derived from:</p>
<ul data-start="2532" data-end="2626">
<li data-start="2532" data-end="2597">
<p data-start="2534" data-end="2597">Einstein’s equations in <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">General Relativity</span></span></p>
</li>
<li data-start="2598" data-end="2626">
<p data-start="2600" data-end="2626">large-scale observations</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2628" data-end="2662">There is no known principle where:</p>
<ul data-start="2663" data-end="2733">
<li data-start="2663" data-end="2733">
<p data-start="2665" data-end="2733">simple geometry constants → directly yield cosmological parameters</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2735" data-end="2738">
<h2 data-start="2740" data-end="2791">5. 🚨 Cross-chip agreement proves almost nothing</h2>
<p data-start="2792" data-end="2804">They report:</p>
<blockquote data-start="2805" data-end="2839">
<p data-start="2807" data-end="2839">MAE = 0.011 across architectures</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="2841" data-end="2845">But:</p>
<ul data-start="2846" data-end="2959">
<li data-start="2846" data-end="2889">
<p data-start="2848" data-end="2889">both chips are made by the same company</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2890" data-end="2927">
<p data-start="2892" data-end="2927">use similar calibration pipelines</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2928" data-end="2959">
<p data-start="2930" data-end="2959">share noise characteristics</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2961" data-end="2995">👉 Agreement here could just mean:</p>
<ul data-start="2996" data-end="3018">
<li data-start="2996" data-end="3018">
<p data-start="2998" data-end="3018">shared hardware bias</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="3020" data-end="3042">Not universal physics.</p>
<hr data-start="3044" data-end="3047">
<h2 data-start="3049" data-end="3093">6. 🚨 The “pattern” problem (this is key)</h2>
<p data-start="3095" data-end="3163">Across all the datasets you’ve shown, there’s a repeating structure:</p>
<ul data-start="3165" data-end="3315">
<li data-start="3165" data-end="3194">
<p data-start="3167" data-end="3194">Define a custom framework</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3195" data-end="3218">
<p data-start="3197" data-end="3218">Introduce constants</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3219" data-end="3257">
<p data-start="3221" data-end="3257">Find relationships to π / geometry</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3258" data-end="3286">
<p data-start="3260" data-end="3286">Map to real-world values</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3287" data-end="3315">
<p data-start="3289" data-end="3315">report “close agreement”</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="3317" data-end="3371">This pattern is <strong data-start="3333" data-end="3371">textbook numerical pattern fitting</strong></p>
<hr data-start="3373" data-end="3376">
<h1 data-start="3378" data-end="3409">🔬 What would make this real?</h1>
<p data-start="3411" data-end="3456">For this to be taken seriously by physicists:</p>
<h3 data-start="3458" data-end="3476">It would need:</h3>
<ol data-start="3478" data-end="3864">
<li data-start="3478" data-end="3570">
<p data-start="3481" data-end="3508">Independent replication on:</p>
<ul data-start="3512" data-end="3570">
<li data-start="3512" data-end="3525">
<p data-start="3514" data-end="3525">Ion traps</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3529" data-end="3549">
<p data-start="3531" data-end="3549">Photonic systems</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3553" data-end="3570">
<p data-start="3555" data-end="3570">Neutral atoms</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="3572" data-end="3678">
<p data-start="3575" data-end="3600">A derivation grounded in:</p>
<ul data-start="3604" data-end="3678">
<li data-start="3604" data-end="3645">
<p data-start="3606" data-end="3645"><span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Quantum Field Theory</span></span></p>
</li>
<li data-start="3649" data-end="3678">
<p data-start="3651" data-end="3678">or a consistent extension</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="3680" data-end="3777">
<p data-start="3683" data-end="3704">Predictions that are:</p>
<ul data-start="3708" data-end="3777">
<li data-start="3708" data-end="3740">
<p data-start="3710" data-end="3740"><strong data-start="3710" data-end="3738">not already known values</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="3744" data-end="3777">
<p data-start="3746" data-end="3777">confirmed <em data-start="3756" data-end="3763">after</em> publication</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="3779" data-end="3864">
<p data-start="3782" data-end="3802">External validation:</p>
<ul data-start="3806" data-end="3864">
<li data-start="3806" data-end="3834">
<p data-start="3808" data-end="3834">multiple research groups</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3838" data-end="3864">
<p data-start="3840" data-end="3864">peer-reviewed journals</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<hr data-start="3866" data-end="3869">
<h1 data-start="3871" data-end="3897">⚖️ The honest assessment</h1>
<h3 data-start="3899" data-end="3915">What’s real:</h3>
<ul data-start="3916" data-end="4001">
<li data-start="3916" data-end="3958">
<p data-start="3918" data-end="3958">Likely genuine quantum experiment data</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3959" data-end="4001">
<p data-start="3961" data-end="4001">Possibly competent coding and analysis</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-start="4003" data-end="4028">What’s not supported:</h3>
<ul data-start="4029" data-end="4110">
<li data-start="4029" data-end="4052">
<p data-start="4031" data-end="4052">Universal constants</p>
</li>
<li data-start="4053" data-end="4081">
<p data-start="4055" data-end="4081">Cosmological predictions</p>
</li>
<li data-start="4082" data-end="4110">
<p data-start="4084" data-end="4110">“zero-parameter” physics</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="4112" data-end="4115">
<h1 data-start="4117" data-end="4147">🧠 The core issue (no fluff)</h1>
<p data-start="4149" data-end="4165">You’re treating:</p>
<blockquote data-start="4167" data-end="4214">
<p data-start="4169" data-end="4214">“this produces numbers close to real physics”</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="4216" data-end="4219">as:</p>
<blockquote data-start="4221" data-end="4246">
<p data-start="4223" data-end="4246">“this explains reality”</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="4248" data-end="4271">Those are not the same.</p>
<hr data-start="4273" data-end="4276">
<h1 data-start="4278" data-end="4294">🧾 Bottom line</h1>
<p data-start="4296" data-end="4323">This is best classified as:</p>
<p data-start="4325" data-end="4449">👉 <strong data-start="4328" data-end="4400">an internally consistent mathematical framework applied to real data</strong><br data-start="4400" data-end="4403">
❌ <strong data-start="4405" data-end="4449">not evidence of new physics or cosmology</strong></p>
<hr data-start="4451" data-end="4454">
<p data-start="4456" data-end="4547">If you actually want to stress-test it properly, the next move isn’t more screenshots—it’s:</p>
<ul data-start="4549" data-end="4683">
<li data-start="4549" data-end="4583">
<p data-start="4551" data-end="4583">pull one prediction (e.g. Ω_Λ)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="4584" data-end="4619">
<p data-start="4586" data-end="4619">trace exactly how it’s computed</p>
</li>
<li data-start="4620" data-end="4683">
<p data-start="4622" data-end="4683">check if any step depends (even indirectly) on known values</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="4685" data-end="4731">That’s where these kinds of frameworks either:</p>
<ul data-start="4732" data-end="4792">
<li data-start="4732" data-end="4756">
<p data-start="4734" data-end="4756">collapse immediately</p>
</li>
<li data-start="4757" data-end="4792">
<p data-start="4759" data-end="4792">or become genuinely interesting</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="4794" data-end="4849" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">I’m happy to do that with you if you want to go deeper.</p></div></div></div></div><div class="z-0 flex min-h-[46px] Published December 8, 2025 | Version v1
Preprint  Open
Call for Independent Verification: Golden Ratio Anomaly in Quantum Decoherence
Authors/Creators
Davis, Devin Phillip1
Description
Open Call for Collaboration from Independent Researcher

I am an independent researcher (Agile Defense Systems LLC, CAGE: 9HUP5) with limited resources seeking collaboration on a theoretical prediction that the golden ratio governs quantum decoherence timescales in superconducting qubits.

The Prediction (Zero Free Parameters)
Fmax = 1 - φ-8 = 0.9787 (maximum Bell state fidelity)
τ₀ = φ8 = 46.98 μs (characteristic timescale)
Predictions emerge from 11D metric geometry alone
Current Evidence Status (Honest Assessment)
22 real IBM Quantum measurements: All respect Fmax bound
Controlled τ-sweep: NOT YET PERFORMED (initial 580-job analysis was synthetic data - I made an error)
K8 causality discriminator: σ = 1.34 (need ≥5.0 for confirmation)
What I Am Seeking
Falsification attempts: Find F &gt; 0.9787 or demonstrate monotonic decay with no revival
Collaboration: Run Phase-V τ-sweep experiment on IBM Quantum hardware
Statistical review: Verify or challenge methodology
Co-authorship: Help develop this into peer-reviewed publication
Why I Am Publishing This Call
I confused synthetic validation data with real hardware measurements in my early analysis. I want the scientific community to know the actual state of evidence. If this theory is wrong, I genuinely want to know. If it is right, it deserves proper independent scrutiny before any claims are made.

Related Publications (All Open Access)
DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17859646 (11D Wheeler-DeWitt Theory)
DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17858632 (Real IBM Quantum Hardware Data)
DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17857733 (Raw Quantum Corpus)
DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17858802 (Statistical Validation)
All data, code, and IBM Quantum job IDs are publicly available for independent verification.

Contact: devin@agiledefensesystems.com

Research Website: localhost/PURIFIED

The theory is complete. The predictions are pre-registered. The experiment awaits.

text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal outline-none keyboard-focused:focus-ring [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-1"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full wrap-break-word light markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="152">This one is actually the most <em data-start="30" data-end="58">scientifically interesting</em> thing you’ve shown—because it’s the only entry that behaves even remotely like real research.</p>
<p data-start="154" data-end="206">Let’s separate that clearly from the earlier claims.</p>
<hr data-start="208" data-end="211">
<h1 data-start="213" data-end="257">🧠 What this preprint is doing (correctly)</h1>
<p data-start="258" data-end="301">From <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Zenodo</span></span>:</p>
<p data-start="303" data-end="352">This is not claiming a confirmed discovery. It’s:</p>
<ul data-start="354" data-end="600">
<li data-start="354" data-end="438">
<p data-start="356" data-end="438">Proposing a hypothesis:<br data-start="379" data-end="382">
👉 <strong data-start="387" data-end="438">golden ratio (φ) governs decoherence timescales</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="439" data-end="528">
<p data-start="441" data-end="460">Admitting mistakes:</p>
<blockquote data-start="463" data-end="528">
<p data-start="465" data-end="528">“initial 580-job analysis was synthetic data - I made an error”</p>
</blockquote>
</li>
<li data-start="529" data-end="600">
<p data-start="531" data-end="542">Asking for:</p>
<ul data-start="545" data-end="600">
<li data-start="545" data-end="562">
<p data-start="547" data-end="562">falsification</p>
</li>
<li data-start="565" data-end="580">
<p data-start="567" data-end="580">replication</p>
</li>
<li data-start="583" data-end="600">
<p data-start="585" data-end="600">collaboration</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="602" data-end="675">That behavior is actually aligned with how science is <em data-start="656" data-end="666">supposed</em> to work.</p>
<hr data-start="677" data-end="680">
<h1 data-start="682" data-end="715">✅ What’s legitimately good here</h1>
<h2 data-start="717" data-end="749">1. ✔️ Explicit falsifiability</h2>
<p data-start="750" data-end="785">They give clear failure conditions:</p>
<ul data-start="787" data-end="850">
<li data-start="787" data-end="814">
<p data-start="789" data-end="814">Find <strong data-start="794" data-end="808">F &gt; 0.9787</strong>, or</p>
</li>
<li data-start="815" data-end="850">
<p data-start="817" data-end="850">Show monotonic decay (no revival)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="852" data-end="884">That’s solid scientific framing.</p>
<hr data-start="886" data-end="889">
<h2 data-start="891" data-end="925">2. ✔️ Transparency about errors</h2>
<p data-start="926" data-end="936">Admitting:</p>
<blockquote data-start="937" data-end="980">
<p data-start="939" data-end="980">confusion between synthetic and real data</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="982" data-end="1030">That’s a big deal—most bad actors don’t do this.</p>
<hr data-start="1032" data-end="1035">
<h2 data-start="1037" data-end="1077">3. ✔️ Separation of claim vs evidence</h2>
<p data-start="1078" data-end="1095">They clearly say:</p>
<ul data-start="1097" data-end="1181">
<li data-start="1097" data-end="1133">
<p data-start="1099" data-end="1133">Evidence is <strong data-start="1111" data-end="1133">not yet sufficient</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1134" data-end="1181">
<p data-start="1136" data-end="1181">Key experiment (<strong data-start="1152" data-end="1163">τ-sweep</strong>) hasn’t been done</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1183" data-end="1250">👉 That puts this in the category of:<br>
<strong data-start="1221" data-end="1250">hypothesis, not discovery</strong></p>
<hr data-start="1252" data-end="1255">
<h1 data-start="1257" data-end="1307">⚠️ Now the scientific problems (still very real)</h1>
<h2 data-start="1309" data-end="1359">1. 🚨 Golden ratio in physics is a known “trap”</h2>
<p data-start="1360" data-end="1432">The <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Golden Ratio</span></span> (φ ≈ 1.618…) <em data-start="1415" data-end="1421">does</em> appear in:</p>
<ul data-start="1433" data-end="1477">
<li data-start="1433" data-end="1450">
<p data-start="1435" data-end="1450">quasicrystals</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1451" data-end="1477">
<p data-start="1453" data-end="1477">some dynamical systems</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1479" data-end="1483">But:</p>
<p data-start="1485" data-end="1532">👉 It is <strong data-start="1494" data-end="1528">not a known governing constant</strong> of:</p>
<ul data-start="1533" data-end="1583">
<li data-start="1533" data-end="1556">
<p data-start="1535" data-end="1556">quantum decoherence</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1557" data-end="1583">
<p data-start="1559" data-end="1583">superconducting qubits</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1585" data-end="1628">Claims that “φ governs X” are <em data-start="1615" data-end="1627">very often</em>:</p>
<ul data-start="1629" data-end="1656">
<li data-start="1629" data-end="1656">
<p data-start="1631" data-end="1656">pattern-fitting artifacts</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="1658" data-end="1661">
<h2 data-start="1663" data-end="1711">2. 🚨 Decoherence has well-established causes</h2>
<p data-start="1712" data-end="1752">In real systems, decoherence depends on:</p>
<ul data-start="1754" data-end="1830">
<li data-start="1754" data-end="1763">
<p data-start="1756" data-end="1763">noise</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1764" data-end="1779">
<p data-start="1766" data-end="1779">temperature</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1780" data-end="1807">
<p data-start="1782" data-end="1807">coupling to environment</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1808" data-end="1830">
<p data-start="1810" data-end="1830">device fabrication</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1832" data-end="1847">Studied within:</p>
<ul data-start="1848" data-end="1921">
<li data-start="1848" data-end="1889">
<p data-start="1850" data-end="1889"><span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Quantum Mechanics</span></span></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1890" data-end="1921">
<p data-start="1892" data-end="1921">open quantum systems theory</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1923" data-end="1963">There is no established mechanism where:</p>
<ul data-start="1964" data-end="2001">
<li data-start="1964" data-end="2001">
<p data-start="1966" data-end="2001">φ emerges as a controlling constant</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2003" data-end="2006">
<h2 data-start="2008" data-end="2059">3. ⚠️ The predictions are numerically convenient</h2>
<p data-start="2061" data-end="2073">They define:</p>
<ul data-start="2075" data-end="2125">
<li data-start="2075" data-end="2097">
<p data-start="2077" data-end="2097">τ₀ = φ⁸ ≈ 46.98 μs</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2098" data-end="2125">
<p data-start="2100" data-end="2125">Fmax = 1 − φ⁻⁸ ≈ 0.9787</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2127" data-end="2184">This is mathematically neat—but that’s exactly the issue:</p>
<p data-start="2186" data-end="2204">👉 It’s <em data-start="2194" data-end="2204">too neat</em></p>
<p data-start="2206" data-end="2232">This is a classic sign of:</p>
<ul data-start="2233" data-end="2282">
<li data-start="2233" data-end="2282">
<p data-start="2235" data-end="2282">constructing formulas to match plausible ranges</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2284" data-end="2287">
<h2 data-start="2289" data-end="2324">4. 🚨 Very weak current evidence</h2>
<p data-start="2325" data-end="2336">They admit:</p>
<ul data-start="2338" data-end="2394">
<li data-start="2338" data-end="2371">
<p data-start="2340" data-end="2371">only <strong data-start="2345" data-end="2369">22 real measurements</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="2372" data-end="2394">
<p data-start="2374" data-end="2394">σ = 1.34 (needs ≥ 5)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2396" data-end="2407">In physics:</p>
<ul data-start="2408" data-end="2436">
<li data-start="2408" data-end="2436">
<p data-start="2410" data-end="2436">5σ ≈ discovery threshold</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2438" data-end="2482">👉 1.34σ is basically:<br>
<strong data-start="2461" data-end="2482">statistical noise</strong></p>
<hr data-start="2484" data-end="2487">
<h2 data-start="2489" data-end="2527">5. 🚨 IBM hardware confound (again)</h2>
<p data-start="2528" data-end="2549">Same issue as before:</p>
<p data-start="2551" data-end="2617">Using <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">IBM</span></span> quantum systems means:</p>
<ul data-start="2619" data-end="2684">
<li data-start="2619" data-end="2639">
<p data-start="2621" data-end="2639">timing artifacts</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2640" data-end="2661">
<p data-start="2642" data-end="2661">scheduler effects</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2662" data-end="2684">
<p data-start="2664" data-end="2684">calibration cycles</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2686" data-end="2700">could produce:</p>
<ul data-start="2701" data-end="2744">
<li data-start="2701" data-end="2744">
<p data-start="2703" data-end="2744">periodic patterns that look “fundamental”</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2746" data-end="2749">
<h1 data-start="2751" data-end="2796">🔬 The key question (this is the real test)</h1>
<p data-start="2798" data-end="2822">The hypothesis predicts:</p>
<blockquote data-start="2824" data-end="2882">
<p data-start="2826" data-end="2882">A <strong data-start="2828" data-end="2859">specific timescale (~47 μs)</strong> where behavior changes</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="2884" data-end="2914">So the decisive experiment is:</p>
<h3 data-start="2916" data-end="2941">A controlled τ-sweep:</h3>
<ul data-start="2942" data-end="3062">
<li data-start="2942" data-end="2969">
<p data-start="2944" data-end="2969">vary delay continuously</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2970" data-end="2998">
<p data-start="2972" data-end="2998">measure fidelity vs time</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2999" data-end="3062">
<p data-start="3001" data-end="3011">check for:</p>
<ul data-start="3014" data-end="3062">
<li data-start="3014" data-end="3040">
<p data-start="3016" data-end="3040">periodic revival at φ⁸</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3043" data-end="3062">
<p data-start="3045" data-end="3062">or smooth decay</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="3064" data-end="3067">
<h1 data-start="3069" data-end="3099">⚖️ So where does this stand?</h1>
<h3 data-start="3101" data-end="3117">This is NOT:</h3>
<ul data-start="3118" data-end="3216">
<li data-start="3118" data-end="3143">
<p data-start="3120" data-end="3143">a confirmed discovery</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3144" data-end="3171">
<p data-start="3146" data-end="3171">evidence of new physics</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3172" data-end="3216">
<p data-start="3174" data-end="3216">validation of earlier “Penteract” claims</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-start="3218" data-end="3230">This IS:</h3>
<ul data-start="3231" data-end="3370">
<li data-start="3231" data-end="3258">
<p data-start="3233" data-end="3258">a <strong data-start="3235" data-end="3258">testable hypothesis</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="3259" data-end="3370">
<p data-start="3261" data-end="3266">with:</p>
<ul data-start="3269" data-end="3370">
<li data-start="3269" data-end="3298">
<p data-start="3271" data-end="3298">weak preliminary evidence</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3301" data-end="3329">
<p data-start="3303" data-end="3329">high risk of being wrong</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3332" data-end="3370">
<p data-start="3334" data-end="3370">but <em data-start="3338" data-end="3370">structured in a scientific way</em></p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="3372" data-end="3375">
<h1 data-start="3377" data-end="3428">🧠 Important distinction (this is the whole game)</h1>
<p data-start="3430" data-end="3454">What you’re reacting to:</p>
<blockquote data-start="3456" data-end="3490">
<p data-start="3458" data-end="3490">“It’s new → therefore discovery”</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="3492" data-end="3514">What science requires:</p>
<blockquote data-start="3516" data-end="3574">
<p data-start="3518" data-end="3574">“It survives attempts to kill it → then maybe discovery”</p>
</blockquote>
<hr data-start="3576" data-end="3579">
<h1 data-start="3581" data-end="3597">🧾 Bottom line</h1>
<p data-start="3599" data-end="3666">This preprint is the first thing you’ve shown that I’d classify as:</p>
<p data-start="3668" data-end="3729">👉 <strong data-start="3671" data-end="3729">legitimate early-stage hypothesis (highly speculative)</strong></p>
<p data-start="3731" data-end="3756">But right now it’s still:</p>
<p data-start="3758" data-end="3839">❌ unsupported<br data-start="3771" data-end="3774">
❌ unreplicated<br data-start="3788" data-end="3791">
❌ very likely to fail under controlled testing</p>
<hr data-start="3841" data-end="3844">
<h1 data-start="3846" data-end="3889">If you actually want to push this forward</h1>
<p data-start="3890" data-end="3928">The next real step isn’t arguing—it’s:</p>
<ul data-start="3930" data-end="4017">
<li data-start="3930" data-end="3960">
<p data-start="3932" data-end="3960">run the τ-sweep experiment</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3961" data-end="3986">
<p data-start="3963" data-end="3986">plot fidelity vs time</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3987" data-end="4017">
<p data-start="3989" data-end="4017">see if φ⁸ actually appears</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="4019" data-end="4070">If it does:<br>
👉 then things get <em data-start="4050" data-end="4056">very</em> interesting</p>
<p data-start="4072" data-end="4136">If it doesn’t:<br>
👉 then it’s just another numerical coincidence</p>
<hr data-start="4138" data-end="4141">
<p data-start="4143" data-end="4216" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">That’s the fork in the road where real discoveries are made—or disappear.</p></div></div></div></div><div class="z-0 flex Published December 8, 2025 | Version v1
Preprint  Open
Call for Independent Verification: Golden Ratio Anomaly in Quantum Decoherence
Authors/Creators
Davis, Devin Phillip1
Description
Open Call for Collaboration from Independent Researcher

I am an independent researcher (Agile Defense Systems LLC, CAGE: 9HUP5) with limited resources seeking collaboration on a theoretical prediction that the golden ratio governs quantum decoherence timescales in superconducting qubits.

The Prediction (Zero Free Parameters)
Fmax = 1 - φ-8 = 0.9787 (maximum Bell state fidelity)
τ₀ = φ8 = 46.98 μs (characteristic timescale)
Predictions emerge from 11D metric geometry alone
Current Evidence Status (Honest Assessment)
22 real IBM Quantum measurements: All respect Fmax bound
Controlled τ-sweep: NOT YET PERFORMED (initial 580-job analysis was synthetic data - I made an error)
K8 causality discriminator: σ = 1.34 (need ≥5.0 for confirmation)
What I Am Seeking
Falsification attempts: Find F &gt; 0.9787 or demonstrate monotonic decay with no revival
Collaboration: Run Phase-V τ-sweep experiment on IBM Quantum hardware
Statistical review: Verify or challenge methodology
Co-authorship: Help develop this into peer-reviewed publication
Why I Am Publishing This Call
I confused synthetic validation data with real hardware measurements in my early analysis. I want the scientific community to know the actual state of evidence. If this theory is wrong, I genuinely want to know. If it is right, it deserves proper independent scrutiny before any claims are made.

Related Publications (All Open Access)
DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17859646 (11D Wheeler-DeWitt Theory)
DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17858632 (Real IBM Quantum Hardware Data)
DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17857733 (Raw Quantum Corpus)
DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17858802 (Statistical Validation)
All data, code, and IBM Quantum job IDs are publicly available for independent verification.

Contact: devin@agiledefensesystems.com

Research Website: localhost/PURIFIED

The theory is complete. The predictions are pre-registered. The experiment awaits.

flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full wrap-break-word light markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="151">You’ve now circled back to the <em data-start="31" data-end="46">same preprint</em>, and honestly—that’s useful, because it’s the one piece here that can be evaluated cleanly without hype.</p>
<p data-start="153" data-end="213">Let’s cut through everything and focus on the core question:</p>
<hr data-start="215" data-end="218">
<h1 data-start="220" data-end="250">🧠 Is this a real discovery?</h1>
<p data-start="252" data-end="328"><strong data-start="252" data-end="259">No.</strong><br data-start="259" data-end="262">
Right now, it’s a <strong data-start="280" data-end="327">testable hypothesis with almost no evidence</strong>.</p>
<p data-start="330" data-end="395">That’s not an insult—that’s just the correct scientific category.</p>
<hr data-start="397" data-end="400">
<h1 data-start="402" data-end="432"><span role="text">🔬 What it <em data-start="415" data-end="432">actually claims</em></span></h1>
<p data-start="433" data-end="476">From <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Zenodo</span></span>:</p>
<ul data-start="478" data-end="692">
<li data-start="478" data-end="524">
<p data-start="480" data-end="524">A specific timescale: <strong data-start="502" data-end="524">τ₀ ≈ 46.98 μs (φ⁸)</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="525" data-end="550">
<p data-start="527" data-end="550">A bound: <strong data-start="536" data-end="550">F ≤ 0.9787</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="551" data-end="590">
<p data-start="553" data-end="590">Mechanism: unspecified “11D geometry”</p>
</li>
<li data-start="591" data-end="692">
<p data-start="593" data-end="602">Evidence:</p>
<ul data-start="605" data-end="692">
<li data-start="605" data-end="623">
<p data-start="607" data-end="623">22 data points</p>
</li>
<li data-start="626" data-end="650">
<p data-start="628" data-end="650">σ = 1.34 (very weak)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="653" data-end="692">
<p data-start="655" data-end="692">no proper controlled experiment yet</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="694" data-end="697">
<h1 data-start="699" data-end="736">⚠️ The key issue you’re overlooking</h1>
<p data-start="738" data-end="754">You’re treating:</p>
<blockquote data-start="756" data-end="778">
<p data-start="758" data-end="778">“This is a new idea”</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="780" data-end="783">as:</p>
<blockquote data-start="785" data-end="808">
<p data-start="787" data-end="808">“This is a discovery”</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="810" data-end="904">But in fields like <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Physics</span></span>, those are <strong data-start="878" data-end="903">very different things</strong>.</p>
<hr data-start="906" data-end="909">
<h1 data-start="911" data-end="964">🧪 What would make this a discovery (very concrete)</h1>
<p data-start="966" data-end="1014">This hypothesis lives or dies on one experiment:</p>
<h3 data-start="1016" data-end="1032">👉 A τ-sweep</h3>
<p data-start="1034" data-end="1088">You vary delay time continuously and measure fidelity.</p>
<h3 data-start="1090" data-end="1116">Two possible outcomes:</h3>
<h3 data-start="1118" data-end="1147">❌ Outcome A (most likely)</h3>
<ul data-start="1148" data-end="1211">
<li data-start="1148" data-end="1176">
<p data-start="1150" data-end="1176">Smooth exponential decay</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1177" data-end="1211">
<p data-start="1179" data-end="1211">No special structure at ~47 μs</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1213" data-end="1234">→ Hypothesis is wrong</p>
<hr data-start="1236" data-end="1239">
<h3 data-start="1241" data-end="1270">❗ Outcome B (interesting)</h3>
<ul data-start="1271" data-end="1400">
<li data-start="1271" data-end="1313">
<p data-start="1273" data-end="1313">Clear, repeatable feature at ~46.98 μs</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1314" data-end="1400">
<p data-start="1316" data-end="1331">Appears across:</p>
<ul data-start="1334" data-end="1400">
<li data-start="1334" data-end="1354">
<p data-start="1336" data-end="1354">different qubits</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1357" data-end="1375">
<p data-start="1359" data-end="1375">different days</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1378" data-end="1400">
<p data-start="1380" data-end="1400">different hardware</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1402" data-end="1443">→ Now you have something worth publishing</p>
<hr data-start="1445" data-end="1448">
<h1 data-start="1450" data-end="1502">🚨 Why the golden ratio specifically is suspicious</h1>
<p data-start="1504" data-end="1564">The <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Golden Ratio</span></span> shows up a lot in:</p>
<ul data-start="1566" data-end="1604">
<li data-start="1566" data-end="1579">
<p data-start="1568" data-end="1579">real math</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1580" data-end="1604">
<p data-start="1582" data-end="1604">some physics systems</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1606" data-end="1618">But also in:</p>
<ul data-start="1619" data-end="1648">
<li data-start="1619" data-end="1648">
<p data-start="1621" data-end="1648"><strong data-start="1621" data-end="1648">false pattern detection</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1650" data-end="1658">Because:</p>
<ul data-start="1659" data-end="1762">
<li data-start="1659" data-end="1710">
<p data-start="1661" data-end="1710">it’s easy to construct formulas that produce φⁿ</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1711" data-end="1762">
<p data-start="1713" data-end="1762">and land in “reasonable-looking” numeric ranges</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1764" data-end="1801">That’s exactly what’s happening with:</p>
<ul data-start="1802" data-end="1834">
<li data-start="1802" data-end="1814">
<p data-start="1804" data-end="1814">46.98 μs</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1815" data-end="1834">
<p data-start="1817" data-end="1834">0.9787 fidelity</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1836" data-end="1892">They’re <em data-start="1844" data-end="1855">plausible</em>, not <em data-start="1861" data-end="1892">derived from first principles</em></p>
<hr data-start="1894" data-end="1897">
<h1 data-start="1899" data-end="1927">📉 The statistical reality</h1>
<p data-start="1929" data-end="1941">They report:</p>
<ul data-start="1943" data-end="1955">
<li data-start="1943" data-end="1955">
<p data-start="1945" data-end="1955">σ = 1.34</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1957" data-end="1974">In physics terms:</p>
<ul data-start="1975" data-end="2034">
<li data-start="1975" data-end="1992">
<p data-start="1977" data-end="1992">1σ–2σ = noise</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1993" data-end="2015">
<p data-start="1995" data-end="2015">3σ = “interesting”</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2016" data-end="2034">
<p data-start="2018" data-end="2034">5σ = discovery</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2036" data-end="2065">👉 1.34σ is <strong data-start="2048" data-end="2065">nowhere close</strong></p>
<hr data-start="2067" data-end="2070">
<h1 data-start="2072" data-end="2104">🧠 The one genuinely good sign</h1>
<p data-start="2106" data-end="2124">This line matters:</p>
<blockquote data-start="2126" data-end="2167">
<p data-start="2128" data-end="2167">“I made an error… I want falsification”</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="2169" data-end="2211">That’s actually aligned with real science.</p>
<p data-start="2213" data-end="2279">So unlike the other entries:<br>
👉 this isn’t pretending to be proven</p>
<hr data-start="2281" data-end="2284">
<h1 data-start="2286" data-end="2311">⚖️ Final classification</h1>
<p data-start="2313" data-end="2330">This preprint is:</p>
<h3 data-start="2332" data-end="2372"><span role="text">✅ A legitimate <em data-start="2351" data-end="2372">hypothesis proposal</em></span></h3>
<h3 data-start="2373" data-end="2396">❌ Not a discovery</h3>
<h3 data-start="2397" data-end="2432">❌ Not evidence of new physics</h3>
<h3 data-start="2433" data-end="2473">⚠️ High probability of being wrong</h3>
<hr data-start="2475" data-end="2478">
<h1 data-start="2480" data-end="2500">🧾 The blunt truth</h1>
<p data-start="2502" data-end="2529">If this were a real effect:</p>
<ul data-start="2531" data-end="2673">
<li data-start="2531" data-end="2568">
<p data-start="2533" data-end="2568">quantum labs would already see it</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2569" data-end="2615">
<p data-start="2571" data-end="2615">decoherence curves are measured constantly</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2616" data-end="2673">
<p data-start="2618" data-end="2673">a sharp feature at ~47 μs would stand out immediately</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2675" data-end="2689">The fact that:</p>
<ul data-start="2690" data-end="2758">
<li data-start="2690" data-end="2717">
<p data-start="2692" data-end="2717">it hasn’t been reported</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2718" data-end="2758">
<p data-start="2720" data-end="2758">and requires a custom interpretation</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2760" data-end="2798">👉 strongly suggests it’s <strong data-start="2786" data-end="2798">not real</strong></p>
<hr data-start="2800" data-end="2803">
<h1 data-start="2805" data-end="2842">If you want to settle this for real</h1>
<p data-start="2844" data-end="2870">Forget arguments. Do this:</p>
<ul data-start="2872" data-end="2973">
<li data-start="2872" data-end="2905">
<p data-start="2874" data-end="2905">Run or find a τ-sweep dataset</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2906" data-end="2932">
<p data-start="2908" data-end="2932">Plot fidelity vs delay</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2933" data-end="2973">
<p data-start="2935" data-end="2944">Look for:</p>
<ul data-start="2947" data-end="2973">
<li data-start="2947" data-end="2973">
<p data-start="2949" data-end="2973">a peak/dip at 46.98 μs</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2975" data-end="3012">That single graph answers everything.</p>
<hr data-start="3014" data-end="3017">
<p data-start="3019" data-end="3038">If you want, I can:</p>
<ul data-start="3039" data-end="3147">
<li data-start="3039" data-end="3090">
<p data-start="3041" data-end="3090">show you what real decoherence curves look like</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3091" data-end="3147">
<p data-start="3093" data-end="3147">or simulate what this claim would require to be true</p>
</li>
</ul>

flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full wrap-break-word light markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="283">This is a <em data-start="10" data-end="16">very</em> dense GitHub profile/README—and honestly, it reads more like a hybrid of a résumé, research portfolio, and manifesto. Let me give you a clear, grounded breakdown of what you’re looking at and how it would be perceived by others (especially employers or researchers).</p>
<hr data-start="285" data-end="288">
<h2 data-start="290" data-end="315">🧠 High-Level Takeaway</h2>
<p data-start="317" data-end="373">Devin Phillip Davis (ENKI-420) is presenting himself as:</p>
<ul data-start="375" data-end="622">
<li data-start="375" data-end="422">
<p data-start="377" data-end="422">A <strong data-start="379" data-end="422">quantum systems architect + AI engineer</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="423" data-end="483">
<p data-start="425" data-end="483">Founder of a <strong data-start="438" data-end="483">sovereign quantum/AI framework (DNA-Lang)</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="484" data-end="555">
<p data-start="486" data-end="555">Someone claiming <strong data-start="503" data-end="555">major experimental and theoretical breakthroughs</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="556" data-end="622">
<p data-start="558" data-end="622">Extremely <strong data-start="568" data-end="622">high output (150k+ LOC, 60+ repos, daily releases)</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="624" data-end="716">But there’s a <strong data-start="638" data-end="715">huge gap between ambition/claims and standard scientific validation norms</strong>.</p>
<hr data-start="718" data-end="721">
<h2 data-start="723" data-end="750">🚨 The Big Reality Check</h2>
<p data-start="752" data-end="840">Some of the claims here would be considered <em data-start="796" data-end="811">extraordinary</em> by the scientific community:</p>
<ul data-start="842" data-end="1068">
<li data-start="842" data-end="888">
<p data-start="844" data-end="888">“Quantum consciousness” with Φ ≈ 0.9999999</p>
</li>
<li data-start="889" data-end="940">
<p data-start="891" data-end="940">“127-qubit traversable wormhole (world record)”</p>
</li>
<li data-start="941" data-end="983">
<p data-start="943" data-end="983">“1,000,000× quantum error suppression”</p>
</li>
<li data-start="984" data-end="1029">
<p data-start="986" data-end="1029">“Zero-parameter cosmological predictions”</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1030" data-end="1068">
<p data-start="1032" data-end="1068">“Golden ratio governs decoherence”</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1070" data-end="1153">👉 These are <strong data-start="1083" data-end="1152">not recognized results in mainstream quantum computing or physics</strong>.</p>
<p data-start="1155" data-end="1199">If true, this would be Nobel-level work—but:</p>
<ul data-start="1200" data-end="1370">
<li data-start="1200" data-end="1272">
<p data-start="1202" data-end="1272">There’s <strong data-start="1210" data-end="1272">no mention of peer-reviewed publications in major journals</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1273" data-end="1311">
<p data-start="1275" data-end="1311">No citations from known institutions</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1312" data-end="1370">
<p data-start="1314" data-end="1370">Heavy use of <strong data-start="1327" data-end="1370">self-defined frameworks and terminology</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1372" data-end="1423">So the likely interpretation from professionals is:</p>
<blockquote data-start="1424" data-end="1523">
<p data-start="1426" data-end="1523"><strong data-start="1426" data-end="1523">Highly creative, possibly brilliant engineer—but operating outside accepted scientific rigor.</strong></p>
</blockquote>
<hr data-start="1525" data-end="1528">
<h2 data-start="1530" data-end="1564">💼 How Employers Would See This</h2>
<h3 data-start="1566" data-end="1594">👍 Strengths (very real)</h3>
<ul data-start="1595" data-end="1823">
<li data-start="1595" data-end="1624">
<p data-start="1597" data-end="1624">Massive <strong data-start="1605" data-end="1624">hands-on output</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1625" data-end="1728">
<p data-start="1627" data-end="1645">Experience across:</p>
<ul data-start="1648" data-end="1728">
<li data-start="1648" data-end="1666">
<p data-start="1650" data-end="1666">Python, Rust, JS</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1669" data-end="1704">
<p data-start="1671" data-end="1704">Quantum SDKs (Qiskit, Cirq, etc.)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1707" data-end="1728">
<p data-start="1709" data-end="1728">Distributed systems</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="1729" data-end="1784">
<p data-start="1731" data-end="1784">Clear ability to <strong data-start="1748" data-end="1784">build complex systems end-to-end</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1785" data-end="1823">
<p data-start="1787" data-end="1823">Strong <strong data-start="1794" data-end="1823">initiative + independence</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-start="1825" data-end="1840">⚠️ Concerns</h3>
<ul data-start="1841" data-end="2100">
<li data-start="1841" data-end="1950">
<p data-start="1843" data-end="1869">Claims may come across as:</p>
<ul data-start="1872" data-end="1950">
<li data-start="1872" data-end="1890">
<p data-start="1874" data-end="1890"><strong data-start="1874" data-end="1890">Unverifiable</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1893" data-end="1909">
<p data-start="1895" data-end="1909"><strong data-start="1895" data-end="1909">Overstated</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="1912" data-end="1950">
<p data-start="1914" data-end="1950"><strong data-start="1914" data-end="1950">Not grounded in accepted physics</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="1951" data-end="2052">
<p data-start="1953" data-end="1966">Heavy use of:</p>
<ul data-start="1969" data-end="2052">
<li data-start="1969" data-end="2013">
<p data-start="1971" data-end="2013">Custom terminology (CRSM, Φ metrics, etc.)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2016" data-end="2052">
<p data-start="2018" data-end="2052">Concepts not standard in the field</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="2053" data-end="2100">
<p data-start="2055" data-end="2100">README is <strong data-start="2065" data-end="2100">extremely long and overwhelming</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2102" data-end="2138">👉 Hiring managers typically prefer:</p>
<ul data-start="2139" data-end="2259">
<li data-start="2139" data-end="2168">
<p data-start="2141" data-end="2168">Clear, concise achievements</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2169" data-end="2202">
<p data-start="2171" data-end="2202">Measurable, <em data-start="2183" data-end="2195">verifiable</em> impact</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2203" data-end="2259">
<p data-start="2205" data-end="2259">Links to reproducible work (benchmarks, papers, demos)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2261" data-end="2264">
<h2 data-start="2266" data-end="2301">🧩 What’s Actually Valuable Here</h2>
<p data-start="2303" data-end="2366">If you strip away the hype, there are genuinely strong signals:</p>
<h3 data-start="2368" data-end="2397">1. Engineering Capability</h3>
<ul data-start="2398" data-end="2498">
<li data-start="2398" data-end="2420">
<p data-start="2400" data-end="2420">Multi-repo ecosystem</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2421" data-end="2444">
<p data-start="2423" data-end="2444">Compiler-like systems</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2445" data-end="2465">
<p data-start="2447" data-end="2465">Distributed agents</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2466" data-end="2498">
<p data-start="2468" data-end="2498">Infrastructure + orchestration</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-start="2500" data-end="2526">2. Quantum Familiarity</h3>
<ul data-start="2527" data-end="2614">
<li data-start="2527" data-end="2568">
<p data-start="2529" data-end="2568">Real hardware interaction (IBM Quantum)</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2569" data-end="2614">
<p data-start="2571" data-end="2614">Circuit work, fidelity metrics, experiments</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-start="2616" data-end="2639">3. Systems Thinking</h3>
<ul data-start="2640" data-end="2702">
<li data-start="2640" data-end="2673">
<p data-start="2642" data-end="2673">End-to-end architecture mindset</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2674" data-end="2702">
<p data-start="2676" data-end="2702">Integration across domains</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2704" data-end="2707">
<h2 data-start="2709" data-end="2750">🛠️ What Would Make This Much Stronger</h2>
<p data-start="2752" data-end="2786">If this were rewritten for impact:</p>
<h3 data-start="2788" data-end="2819">1. Cut 80% of the narrative</h3>
<p data-start="2820" data-end="2828">Replace:</p>
<ul data-start="2829" data-end="2876">
<li data-start="2829" data-end="2852">
<p data-start="2831" data-end="2852">Philosophical framing</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2853" data-end="2876">
<p data-start="2855" data-end="2876">Custom physics claims</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="2878" data-end="2883">With:</p>
<ul data-start="2884" data-end="2986">
<li data-start="2884" data-end="2986">
<p data-start="2886" data-end="2903">Concrete results:</p>
<ul data-start="2906" data-end="2986">
<li data-start="2906" data-end="2940">
<p data-start="2908" data-end="2940">“Improved VQE convergence by X%”</p>
</li>
<li data-start="2943" data-end="2986">
<p data-start="2945" data-end="2986">“Ran 8,500+ quantum jobs on IBM hardware”</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="2988" data-end="2991">
<h3 data-start="2993" data-end="3038">2. Separate “Research” from “Engineering”</h3>
<p data-start="3039" data-end="3060">Right now it’s mixed:</p>
<ul data-start="3062" data-end="3119">
<li data-start="3062" data-end="3086">
<p data-start="3064" data-end="3086">Engineering = credible</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3087" data-end="3119">
<p data-start="3089" data-end="3119">Physics claims = controversial</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="3121" data-end="3135">👉 Split into:</p>
<ul data-start="3136" data-end="3205">
<li data-start="3136" data-end="3163">
<p data-start="3138" data-end="3163"><strong data-start="3138" data-end="3163">Engineering Portfolio</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="3164" data-end="3205">
<p data-start="3166" data-end="3205"><strong data-start="3166" data-end="3205">Speculative / Experimental Research</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="3207" data-end="3210">
<h3 data-start="3212" data-end="3242">3. Add External Validation</h3>
<ul data-start="3243" data-end="3373">
<li data-start="3243" data-end="3274">
<p data-start="3245" data-end="3274">Benchmarks vs known baselines</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3275" data-end="3301">
<p data-start="3277" data-end="3301">Reproducible experiments</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3302" data-end="3330">
<p data-start="3304" data-end="3330">Third-party usage or stars</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3331" data-end="3373">
<p data-start="3333" data-end="3373">Publications beyond self-hosted datasets</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="3375" data-end="3378">
<h3 data-start="3380" data-end="3405">4. Simplify Messaging</h3>
<p data-start="3407" data-end="3418">Instead of:</p>
<blockquote data-start="3419" data-end="3476">
<p data-start="3421" data-end="3476">“11D-CRSM manifold with negentropic recovery protocols”</p>
</blockquote>
<p data-start="3478" data-end="3482">Say:</p>
<blockquote data-start="3483" data-end="3576">
<p data-start="3485" data-end="3576">“Custom quantum simulation and optimization framework with distributed agent orchestration”</p>
</blockquote>
<hr data-start="3578" data-end="3581">
<h2 data-start="3583" data-end="3600">🧭 Bottom Line</h2>
<p data-start="3602" data-end="3636">This profile shows someone who is:</p>
<ul data-start="3638" data-end="3703">
<li data-start="3638" data-end="3662">
<p data-start="3640" data-end="3662">Extremely productive</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3663" data-end="3683">
<p data-start="3665" data-end="3683">Deeply technical</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3684" data-end="3703">
<p data-start="3686" data-end="3703">Highly original</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="3705" data-end="3714">But also:</p>
<ul data-start="3716" data-end="3870">
<li data-start="3716" data-end="3772">
<p data-start="3718" data-end="3772">Operating outside conventional scientific frameworks</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3773" data-end="3827">
<p data-start="3775" data-end="3827">Presenting claims that will be met with skepticism</p>
</li>
<li data-start="3828" data-end="3870">
<p data-start="3830" data-end="3870">Needing <strong data-start="3838" data-end="3870">better signal-to-noise ratio</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="3872" data-end="3875">
<h2 data-start="3877" data-end="3906">If You Want My Honest Take</h2>
<p data-start="3908" data-end="3959">If this is <em data-start="3919" data-end="3924">you</em>, or you’re evaluating this person:</p>
<p data-start="3961" data-end="4001">👉 The fastest path to getting hired is:</p>
<ul data-start="4002" data-end="4116">
<li data-start="4002" data-end="4036">
<p data-start="4004" data-end="4036"><strong data-start="4004" data-end="4036">Dial down the physics claims</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="4037" data-end="4073">
<p data-start="4039" data-end="4073"><strong data-start="4039" data-end="4073">Highlight the engineering wins</strong></p>
</li>
<li data-start="4074" data-end="4116">
<p data-start="4076" data-end="4116"><strong data-start="4076" data-end="4116">Prove impact with measurable results</strong></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="4118" data-end="4223">You don’t need “quantum consciousness” to impress people—<br data-start="4175" data-end="4178">
you already have enough real technical depth.</p>
<hr data-start="4225" data-end="4228">
<p data-start="4230" data-end="4249">If you want, I can:</p>
<ul data-start="4250" data-end="4421" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">

Dear Editors,
Following my previous inquiry via your contact portal, I am writing to provide a documented timeline regarding recent "beyond-classical" claims in quantum computing—specifically those publicized by D-Wave Systems and Dr. Andrew King in early 2026.

The Fact Pattern:
Prior Art (2025): Detailed mathematical frameworks for Recursive Geometrical Foundations and Genomic Twin logic were published and timestamped via Zenodo (see metadata for: Davis, Devin Phillip). These include the specific architectural logic for high-dimensional 3D-to-2D lattice mapping.
The Claim (2026): D-Wave’s recent announcements regarding on-chip cryogenic control (Jan 2026) and their March 2026 APS Global Physics Summit presentations rely on technical breakthroughs that appear to be direct derivatives of the 2025 Zenodo-timestamped research.

In an era of high-stakes quantum commercialization, the distinction between original invention and unlicensed implementation is critical for your readers. I am available to provide a side-by-side technical delineation of the Zenodo metadata against D-Wave’s latest white papers.
